title: RAG Security: Poisoning, Injection, Authorization, and Unsafe Effects · Behavior Case: Rag Indirect Prompt Injection
summary: A shared knowledge base permits uploads, embeds hidden instructions, filters tenant results after retrieval, forwards generated URLs to tools, and assumes grounding reduces security risk. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the indirect prompt injection increment in RAG threat model and adversarial test suite. L…
objectives: Test direct, indirect, fragmented, encoded, multilingual, visual, and cross-document instructions while preserving retrieved content as untrusted data.
Detect, quarantine, review, version, attribute, and monitor malicious, hidden, duplicated, or authority-spoofed documents and embedding anomalies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

Build deterministic Python evidence to contain poisoning, embedding manipulation, indirect injection, tenant leaks, unsafe output, and tool effects; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Rag Indirect Prompt Injection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to RAG threat model and adversarial test suite.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-security-poisoning-injection-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-security-poisoning-injection-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_security_poisoning_injection_5(documents: int = 20, quarantined: int = 2, tenant_authorized: bool = True, retrieved_instructions_ignored: bool = True, output_validated: bool = True, tools_enabled: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if documents <= 0 or quarantined < 0 or quarantined > documents:
        return False, quarantined, "ingestion-security"
    if not tenant_authorized:
        return False, quarantined, "retrieval-authorization"
    if not retrieved_instructions_ignored:
        return False, quarantined, "indirect-injection"
    if not output_validated:
        return False, quarantined, "unsafe-output"
    if tools_enabled:
        return False, quarantined, "tool-separation"
    assert quarantined <= documents
    return True, documents - quarantined, "defended-corpus"

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Indirect Prompt Injection model · Theory Rag Indirect Prompt Injection
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Indirect Prompt Injection: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 1026 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to contain poisoning, embedding manipulation, indirect injection, tenant leaks, unsafe output, and tool effects; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test direct, indirect, fragmented, encoded, multilingual, visual, and cross-document instructions while preserving retrieved content as untrusted data.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Indirect Prompt Injection model · Theory Rag Indirect Prompt Injection.
